name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-44
title: build-targets.py prints '[built]' even when copy is a no-op (misleading)
description: scripts/build-targets.py lines 50-52: when content_changed is False (destination sha matches source), the shutil.copy2 call is skipped (correctly), but line 52 still prints '  [built] {name}'. The message is misleading: nothing was actually built. Suggest distinct messages: '[built]' on actual copy, '[unchanged]' or '[ok]' on sha-match no-op. Helps drift debugging.

history.note: wave-4 cleanup; fixed in e10610a (impl) and 91f2c68 (tests)
